assertions.reason: On the isolated native Claude page, the list surface showed exactly the three source-filter tabs `全部`, `用户来源`, and `市场来源` above the Claude search box, with no extra Claude-specific route or platform entry required.

The active tab changed the visible Claude rows by source before search: `全部` showed marketplace `other-skill`, marketplace `shared-skill`, and user `shared-skill`; `用户来源` reduced the list to only the user `shared-skill`; `市场来源` reduced the list to only marketplace `other-skill` and marketplace `shared-skill`.

While `市场来源` was selected, entering `shared` in the Claude list search field returned only the marketplace `shared-skill` row. The hidden user-source duplicate did not reappear, proving search stayed scoped to the active source tab instead of re-surfacing rows excluded by the tab filter.

frictions: The already-running native `skills-manage` app was using the real `/Users/happypeet` HOME instead of the assigned fixture HOME, so I launched a dedicated isolated Tauri window against the already-healthy `http://localhost:24200` dev URL before grading the assertions.
The Claude page search box did not expose a distinct AX title, so I targeted it through AX placeholder metadata and set the query via AX value assignment rather than relying on keyboard input.
notes: Direct native evidence was captured from the isolated Tauri app launched with `HOME=/tmp/skills-manage-test-fixtures/claude-multi-source`; the archived `.factory/library/claude-source-tab-*.png` fallback screenshots were not needed for final grading. No app-specific runtime failures were observed during the validated flow; the isolated launch log only showed a single macOS input-method warning (`IMKCFRunLoopWakeUpReliable`).
